hero_subtitle: A historic and spiritual sacred place in Jamalpur
about_text: Doyamoyee Temple, located at Zero Point in Jamalpur, is a historic Hindu temple established in 1698 by Sri Krishna Roy Chowdhury during the reign of Nawab Murshid Quli Khan. The complex includes Shiva, Kali, Nat Mandir, and Manasa Devi shrines and is renowned for centuries-old murals.
history_text: This 321-year-old temple hosts daily worship and draws devotees from across the country during the annual Ashtami Fair. The temple is open from 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM.
events_text: Doyamoyee Temple holds Durga Puja, Ashtami Fair, and annual Chaitra festivals. Each event will feature a countdown timer.
volunteer_text: If you wish to participate as a volunteer at Doyamoyee Temple, please fill out the form below.
notice_text: View recent announcements, puja schedules, and important notifications here.
gallery_text: Photo gallery showcasing the temple's beauty and centuries-old murals.
